title: Stop wrapping pull messages
body:
The length and size of our error messages on failure to pull
is huge.  This patch at least eliminates some of the wrapping.
But I think eventually we need to look at containers/image
and see if we can modify the error messages to something a little
more human friendly.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Sure but do we need to return all of the HTML Information. ``` podman pull quay.io/buildah1 Error: error pulling image "quay.io/buildah1": unable to pull quay.io/buildah1: unable to pull image: Error initializing source docker://quay.io/buildah1:latest: Error reading manifest latest in quay.io/buildah1: error parsing HTTP 404 response body: invalid character '<' looking for beginning of value: "\n404 Not Found\n

Not Found

\n

The requested URL was not found on the server. If you entered the URL manually please check your spelling and try again.

\n" ``` is not exactly human friendly.
